hypothesis: If 3D molecule generators are trained with chemically accurate, aromatic-aware valency constraints (or on kekulized graphs) and evaluated using GFN2-xTB, then they will produce significantly fewer valency violations and smaller Δr/Δθ/Δφ with near-zero ΔE_relax than models trained on the original bugged GEOM-Drugs and/or evaluated with MMFF94, because correcting aromatic valences prevents invalid bonding and GFN2-xTB aligns with the dataset’s quantum-optimized geometries.
